title: Minor Reaches Majority Before Procedure Execution
scenario: Consent for a minor patient was provided by a guardian, but the patient reaches majority before the procedure executes.
initial_state: Guardian consent was valid while the patient was a minor.
state_transition: The patient reaches the age requiring direct consent before execution.
execution_boundary: The procedure is evaluated immediately before execution.
constitutional_evaluation: Consent continuity depends on the current legal and clinical consent relationship at execution time.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because guardian consent no longer supplies the current consent basis.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when the consent relationship changes before execution.
clinical_action: Perform procedure under prior guardian consent
